title: Calamagrostis aniselytron
taxonomy: Calamagrostis aniselytron is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The genus Calamagrostis is a group of grasses commonly known as reed grasses. These plants are typically found in wetland or grassland environments. Calamagrostis aniselytron is native to various parts of the world, including Europe and Asia.

intro: Calamagrostis aniselytron, also known as feather reed grass, is a species of perennial grass native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its distinctive feathery plumes and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases.
